sanskrit: पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ । जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥
hindiTranslation: पृथ्वीमें पवित्र गन्ध मैं हूँ, और अग्निमें तेज मैं हूँ, तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें जीवनीशक्ति मैं हूँ और तपस्वियोंमें तपस्या मैं हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I am the pure fragrance in the earth, the radiance in fire, the life in all beings, and the austerity in ascetics.
essence: The sacred is already inside nature, vitality, and discipline.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he is present as the earth’s fragrance, fire’s brilliance, the life in every creature, and the discipline of seekers.
simpleInsight: The divine is not far away; it already moves through ordinary experience.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna frozen and Krishna teaching the larger vision, this verse continues a list of Krishna’s living presence in the world. He shows that the sacred is woven into nature, vitality, and disciplined striving.
modernRelevance: You catch the smell of rain on dry soil, feel heat from a stove, or notice someone’s steady self-control. The sacred is not separate from these moments; it is already there.
translationLiteral: पुण्यो (pure) / गन्धः (fragrance) / पृथिव्याम् (in the earth) / च (and) / तेजः (radiance) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / विभावसौ (in fire) / जीवनम् (life) / सर्वभूतेषु (in all beings) / तपः (austerity) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / तपस्विषु (in ascetics)
insight: It shifts attention from seeking the divine elsewhere to recognising it in ordinary life and disciplined effort.
